docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This is the collected knowledge for the Lodge 100 3 area grow setup

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• docker compose up -d - Serve the project.
  • docker run --rm -it -v ${PWD}:/docs squidfunk/mkdocs-material build - Build the docs.
  • docker run --rm -it -v ~/.ssh:/root/.ssh:ro -v ~/.ssh/config.docker:/root/.ssh/config:ro -v ${PWD}:/docs squidfunk/mkdocs-material gh-deploy - deploy docs to github pages

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Minimum Setup
docs.text:
I would at least reccommend an ESP8266 flashed with tasmota. I use a ESP32 myself because it offers much more I/O, is way faster, has an USB-C connection and is absolutely worth the price!

flashed with tasmota32 (custom build that includes the MLX90614 library)

docs.title: Growlights (206W)
docs.text: The growlights are all LEDs and use 24VDC (except the cutlings light which is 230V). Keep in mind that there is a difference between growth and bloom lights - the growth lights are more blueish and the bloom lights are more reddish.

docs.title: 60x60x160 area (main chamber)
docs.text:
This is the left area - the main area (basically a DS60 tent)

  • 20W IR + 20W UV supplement (COL20IR + COL20UV)

docs.title: When in flowering period
docs.text: • 5x 20W Cosmorrow Bloom Kit (COM100FS)

docs.title: When in vegetative period
docs.text: • 2x 20W Cosmorrow Grow Kit (TNO40GR)

docs.title: 60x40x100 area (mother plant)
docs.text:
• 20W Cosmorrow Grow (COP2065)
  • 20W Infrared (COL20IR)

docs.title: 60x40x60 area (aeroponic cloner)
docs.text: • Factory TLED V2.0 26W 6500K Growth (link)

docs.title: Power consumption
docs.text:
I'm using a smart plug that supports measuring the total power consumption of the grow setup and is also Tasmota compatible: Tasmota Steckdose NOUS A1T

An example payload would look like this:

{
  "Time": "2024-07-24T22:24:32",
  "ENERGY": {
    "TotalStartTime": "2024-01-04T15:00:40",
    "Total": 279.919,
    "Yesterday": 2.499,
    "Today": 2.29,
    "Period": 11,
    "Power": 126,
    "ApparentPower": 178,
    "ReactivePower": 125,
    "Factor": 0.71,
    "Voltage": 296,
    "Current": 0.601
  }
}

// TODO: add details how much energy the system consumes in different modes (veg/bloom, aero cloner running or not)

docs.title: Relais and Switches
docs.text: Can be used for various grow LEDs, fans, pumps, extractors, etc… (also suitable for 230VAC). I use this one from Elegoo one here with 8 channels. Additionally I got some snubbers (link here) to eliminate high voltage spikes caused by switching on inductive loads (currently the 2 pumps)

docs.title: Temperature Sensors
docs.text:
• MLX90614: infrared leaf temperature for VPD calculation (I2C), see Tasmota documentation for MLX90614. Warning: you need to compile the binary of Tasmota yourself because it doesn't include this feature in the default binaries.
  • 3x DS18B20: for water temperature (analog Sensor), look at Tasmota documentation for more infos and how to connect multiple sensors to one GPIO pin.

docs.title: Temperature/Humidity Sensors
docs.text: • 2x BME280: temp, humidity and barometric pressure (I2C), see Tasmota documentation for BME280.

docs.title: TDS/EC Sensor
docs.text: • QCRobot TDS meter sensor, see Tamota documentation for Analog Pins.

docs.title: Tasmota Pump Rules
docs.text: See Tasmota rules documentation for more info (rules can also be set via MQTT).

docs.title: Rule for Aeroponic Cloner Pump (POWER1)
docs.text: • Rule1 ON Power1#Boot DO backlog Power1 ON; RuleTimer1 60 ENDON ON Rules#Timer=1 DO backlog Power1 OFF; RuleTimer2 240 ENDON ON Rules#Timer=2 DO backlog Power1 ON; RuleTimer1 60 ENDON Rule1 1

docs.title: Rule for Aeroponic Main Setup Pump (POWER2)
docs.text: • Rule1 ON Power1#Boot DO backlog Power1 ON; RuleTimer1 60 ENDON ON Rules#Timer=1 DO backlog Power1 OFF; RuleTimer2 240 ENDON ON Rules#Timer=2 DO backlog Power1 ON; RuleTimer1 60 ENDON Rule1 1

docs.title: Wiring of the electronics
docs.text: We need to take into account which cable dimensions we use for different loads - most importantly here is the 100W grow light that we will use.

docs.title: Cable dimensions
docs.text: 0,75 mm² (18 AWG) is enough for 12A which which is enough for all the lights (100W/24V=4,16A). I use this set for wiring - features 3 colors and that's enough.

docs.title: Recommended connection terminals
docs.text:
• Split L, N and PE wires: 3 in, 6 out
  • Connect anything up to 3 wires: 3 in, 3 out
  • Distribute 24V/5V with GND: 2 in, 8 out
  • Distribute 5V/GND: 1 in, 5 out
  • 230VAC in: Power plug terminal

docs.title: JST/Dupont connectors
docs.text: For the sensors and other devices that require 5V we will use JST/Dupont connectors. This box should be enough for the beginning. Also consider getting a few meters of ribbon cable to connect all the low voltage peripherals.

docs.title: DC sockets (5.5mm x 2.1mm)
docs.text: To connect the lights and the ventilation we will use DC sockets that are connected to the relais

docs.title: Pumps (~20W)
docs.text: 1 pump each for the aeroponic main system, the aeroponic cloner and the aeration of the nutrient solution for the mother plant.

docs.title: Aero Main Pump
docs.text: QWORK® Mini Wasserpumpen Ultraleise Tauchpumpe - 800 L/H – 15 W

docs.title: Airstone Pump
docs.text: Hailea ACO-2201 Air Pump 1.8 W

docs.title: Extraction
docs.text:
• DF16E150 15W
  • DF16E350 35W

docs.title: Air flow
docs.text: 2x MONKEY FAN 24V MF2X120 2.8W each (5.7W in total)

docs.title: AC-DC Converter
docs.text: The converter used for 230VAC to 24VDC (to power grow LEDs + ventilation) is this one from DUSKTEC that has 300W which is enough for lights&co.

docs.title: DC-DC Converter
docs.text: The ESP32 and (some) sensors require 5V to operate. The power supply that we have is 24V. To convert the 24V to 5V we will use a DC-DC converter. The converter that we will use is this one from Bauer Electronics.

docs.title: Important things to know
docs.text:
• UV/IR supplement lights - how and why
  • Leaf VPD

const leafVPD = 0.61078 * Math.exp((msg.payload.MLX90614.OBJTMP / (msg.payload.MLX90614.OBJTMP + 237.3) * 17.2694)) - 0.61078 * Math.exp((msg.payload.BME280.Temperature / (msg.payload.BME280.Temperature + 237.3) * 17.2694)) * msg.payload.BME280.Humidity / 100.0;

  • Cannabis problem guide

docs.title: pH/EC measurement
docs.text:
If you choose to grow with (DWC) hydroponics or aeroponics you need to monitor your pH and EC levels. This is crucial for the health of your plants. The pH level of your nutrient solution determines how well your plants can absorb nutrients. The EC level of your nutrient solution determines how much nutrients are available to your plants.

If you want to get started right away I can recommend you this Multiparameter Tester from Ueomul.

docs.title: Base Nutrients
docs.text: I'm using the plagron Hydro A+B for my nutrient solution according to following grow schedule (if longer vegetative phase wanted, just repeat week 2)

docs.title: Additives
docs.text:
• Hydro Roots to get more roots
  • Power Buds to get more buds
  • Green Sensation 4 in one: more dense buds, heavier buds, more pest resistance and better nutrient intake

docs.title: Growbox configuration
docs.text:
you are pretty free to use whatever you like here - I'm using the left 60x60 area as the main box for 4 plants. The mother plant is in the right lower part and the cloner in the right upper part (both 60x40 area).

I'm also using 4x 24VDC Monkey Fans - 2 in the big area, and 1 each in the 60x40 areas.

docs.title: Mother plant
docs.text: Growrilla DWC Hydro 19l. Used with an airstone powered by the Hailea ACO 2210 (78l/h) pump.

docs.title: Cloner
docs.text: Nutriculture X-Stream Plant Aeroponic Unheated Propagator

docs.title: Main Setup
docs.text: Platinum Aero Star 40x40 with upgraded Pump (QWORK® Mini Wasserpumpen Ultraleise Tauchpumpe - 800 L/H – 15 W) + 360° nozzles

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
TODO: add traefik config in compose file too (or maybe provide one without it)

services:
  mosquitto:
    image: eclipse-mosquitto:latest
    container_name: mosquitto
    restart: always
    ports:
      - "1883:1883"
    volumes:
      - mosquitto_data:/mosquitto/data
      - mosquitto_logs:/mosquitto/log
      - ./config:/mosquitto/config
    command: mosquitto -c /mosquitto/config/mosquitto.conf
    networks:
      - iot

  telegraf:
    image: telegraf:latest
    container_name: telegraf
    restart: always
    volumes:
      - ./telegraf.conf:/etc/telegraf/telegraf.conf:ro
    networks:
      - iot
    depends_on:
      - mosquitto
      - influxdb

  influxdb:
    image: influxdb:latest
    restart: always
    container_name: influxdb
    volumes:
      - influxdb_data:/var/lib/influxdb2
      - influxdb_config:/etc/influxdb2
    networks:
      - iot
      - back-tier
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.docker.network=traefik"
      - "traefik.frontend.rule=Host:<your.influxdb.host>"
      - "traefik.port=8086"
    environment:
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_MODE=setup
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_USERNAME=admin
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_PASSWORD=<your-password>
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_ORG=ludi
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_BUCKET=mqtt
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_ADMIN_TOKEN=<your-admin-token>

  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana
    container_name: grafana
    restart: always
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.docker.network=traefik"
      - "traefik.frontend.rule=Host:<your.grafana.host>"
      - "traefik.port=3000"
    networks:
      - iot
      - back-tier
    volumes:
      - ./grafana-provisioning:/etc/grafana/provisioning
      - grafana-data:/var/lib/grafana
    environment:
      GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD: <your-admin-password>
    depends_on:
      - influxdb

  node-red:
    image: nodered/node-red:latest
    container_name: node-red
    restart: always
    ports:
      - "1880:1880"
    volumes:
      - node_red_data:/data
    networks:
      - iot
      - back-tier
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.docker.network=traefik"
      - "traefik.frontend.rule=Host:<your.node-red.host>"
      - "traefik.port=1880"
      - "traefik.frontend.entryPoints=https"
      - "traefik.frontend.redirect.entryPoint=https"
      - "traefik.frontend.redirect.permanent=true"
    environment:
      - TZ=Europe/Vienna

networks:
  iot:
  back-tier:
    external:
      name: traefik

volumes:
  mosquitto_data:
  mosquitto_logs:
  influxdb_data:
  influxdb_config:
  grafana-data:
  node_red_data:
